prompt:
Passage 1
A manufacturing company recently installed new energy-efficient lighting throughout its facility. The upgrade replaced older fluorescent fixtures with LED lights. According to company records, the new system uses approximately 40 percent less electricity than the previous system.
In addition to reducing energy consumption, the LED fixtures require less maintenance because they last significantly longer. Facility managers estimate that maintenance costs related to lighting will decrease over the next several years.
Employees reported another benefit after the installation. Many workers stated that the brighter and more consistent lighting improved visibility on the production floor. Supervisors noted fewer complaints about eye strain and difficulty reading labels and instructions.
Although the initial installation cost was substantial, company leaders expect the project to pay for itself through lower utility bills and reduced maintenance expenses.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: The company is planning additional building renovations.
choices.B: The company replaced its lighting system and expects multiple benefits.
choices.C: Employees requested higher wages after the lighting upgrade.
choices.D: Fluorescent lights are safer than LED lights.

explanation: The passage focuses on the company's decision to replace its lighting system and the resulting benefits, including lower energy use, reduced maintenance costs, and improved visibility. The other choices mention details not supported by the passage.

prompt:
Passage 2
MEMO
To: All Employees
Subject: Parking Lot Resurfacing
Beginning Monday, July 14, the employee parking lot will undergo resurfacing. Construction is expected to continue for approximately two weeks.
During this period, employees should use the temporary parking area located behind Warehouse B. Signs will be posted to direct traffic. Employees are encouraged to arrive a few minutes early because additional walking time may be required.
The resurfacing project is being completed to repair damaged pavement and improve safety. Please use caution when entering and leaving the work area.
Thank you for your cooperation during this project.

What is the primary purpose of the memo?

choices.A: To announce a new warehouse opening
choices.B: To explain changes related to a parking lot project
choices.C: To request employee feedback about safety

explanation: The memo informs employees about parking changes caused by a resurfacing project and provides instructions during construction.

prompt:
Passage 3
For many years, companies stored business records primarily on paper. Documents were kept in filing cabinets and archives that often required large amounts of storage space. Finding a specific record could take considerable time, especially when records were several years old.
Today, many organizations use digital recordkeeping systems. Electronic records can be searched quickly using keywords or document numbers. Digital storage also reduces the need for physical filing space.
However, digital systems present their own challenges. Organizations must protect electronic information from unauthorized access and ensure that backup copies are available in case of equipment failure. As a result, many businesses invest heavily in cybersecurity and data protection measures.

What is the passage mainly about?

choices.B: The advantages and challenges of digital recordkeeping
choices.C: Why businesses should avoid electronic systems

explanation: The passage discusses the benefits of digital recordkeeping as well as the challenges associated with protecting electronic information.

prompt:
Passage 4
In 1903, two brothers achieved a milestone that changed transportation history. After years of experimentation, they successfully completed a powered flight in a machine they had designed and built. The flight lasted only a short time and covered a relatively small distance, but it demonstrated that controlled, powered flight was possible.
The brothers continued improving their designs. Over the following years, aircraft became more reliable and capable of traveling greater distances. Inventors and engineers around the world built upon these early achievements.
Today, air travel plays a major role in transportation and commerce. Millions of passengers travel by air each year, and aircraft move goods quickly across long distances. The modern aviation industry traces its origins to those early experiments.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.B: Early successful powered flight helped launch the modern aviation industry.
choices.D: Modern aircraft are identical to early airplanes.

explanation: The passage explains how an early successful powered flight became the foundation for the modern aviation industry.

prompt:
Passage 5
A regional utility company recently launched a program designed to help customers reduce household energy consumption. Participants receive information about common sources of energy waste and practical steps that can improve efficiency.
One recommendation is to replace older incandescent bulbs with LED bulbs. Another is to seal gaps around doors and windows to reduce heating and cooling losses. Customers are also encouraged to unplug electronics that are not in use, since some devices continue to consume electricity even when turned off.
According to company representatives, small changes made consistently over time can result in meaningful reductions in energy usage. The company hopes the program will help customers lower utility bills while also reducing overall demand on the electrical system.

What is the primary purpose of the program described in the passage?

choices.B: To help customers reduce energy consumption

explanation: The program's purpose is to help customers reduce household energy consumption through practical efficiency measures.

prompt:
Passage 6
Safety inspections are a routine part of many workplaces. These inspections help identify hazards before accidents occur. During an inspection, equipment may be examined for signs of wear, safety procedures may be reviewed, and work areas may be checked for potential risks.
Some employees view inspections as interruptions to their normal duties. However, safety professionals emphasize that inspections play an important role in preventing injuries and reducing costly downtime. Problems discovered during an inspection can often be corrected before they lead to larger issues.
Organizations that conduct regular inspections often develop a stronger culture of safety because employees become more aware of workplace hazards and prevention strategies.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.B: Safety inspections help identify and prevent workplace hazards.
choices.C: Employees should conduct inspections only after accidents occur.

explanation: The passage focuses on how safety inspections help identify hazards and prevent workplace accidents.

prompt:
Passage 7
Many communities operate recycling programs to reduce the amount of waste sent to landfills. Materials such as paper, cardboard, glass, aluminum, and certain plastics can often be collected, processed, and used to create new products.
Recycling offers several benefits. It can reduce the demand for raw materials, conserve energy, and decrease the amount of space required for waste disposal. However, recycling programs are most effective when residents understand and follow local guidelines regarding acceptable materials.
Contamination occurs when non-recyclable items are placed in recycling containers. Contaminated loads may require additional sorting or, in some cases, may be rejected entirely. For this reason, public education remains an important part of successful recycling efforts.

What is the passage mainly about?

choices.B: The benefits and challenges of recycling programs
choices.D: Waste disposal regulations for businesses only

explanation: The passage discusses both the advantages of recycling and the challenges caused by contamination.

prompt:
Passage 8
A company recently introduced a new employee training program for workers hired within the past year. The program combines classroom instruction with hands-on practice. Managers developed the training after reviewing employee feedback and performance data.
The classroom portion focuses on company procedures, workplace communication, and safety requirements. During the hands-on portion, employees work alongside experienced staff members who demonstrate job tasks and answer questions.
Company leaders believe the program will shorten the learning curve for new employees and improve overall job performance. Early results suggest that participants are becoming productive more quickly than previous groups of new hires.

Why was the training program developed?

choices.B: After managers reviewed feedback and performance data

explanation: Managers created the training program after reviewing employee feedback and performance data.
